source_reviews.notes: CMS directly supports the 85% payment rate for qualifying PTA/OTA-furnished services and the CQ/CO modifier mechanics. It demonstrates a real Medicare payer channel, but Medicare does not cover every payer or subsegment and does not numerically establish the selected 0.30 capture value.

The vendor survey release directly supports growing AI interest in documentation, billing, compliance, front-desk and scheduling workflows and a goal of reducing administrative burden. It is directional vendor-survey evidence and does not measure the precise replaceable wage share, 20% effective adoption, four-year t50, or productivity conversion.

APTA directly reports a 9.5% national vacancy rate for outpatient physical-therapy practices and approximately 13% of PT/PTA positions open. This supports staffing scarcity and a licensed-labor constraint, but it does not quantify clinic moat or owner succession.

The paper directly supports fragmentation, numerous national platforms and historical private PT transactions at 10.4x-16.1x EBITDA for $12M-$175M EBITDA targets. It supports active consolidation direction and a platform-scale exit anchor, but the transaction targets are materially larger than the intended band and do not directly establish 4.5x entry or 10.0x exit values for a smaller clinic group.

The company release is accessible and reports a consultant-validated claims methodology, reduced utilization including physical or occupational therapy, and 2.4x ROI. It is used in the terminal-demand narrative but is not referenced by a finalized score input, so it is correctly marked not score-driving.

The APTA page identifies a 2025 workforce-demographics report covering age and other workforce characteristics. It does not report private-practice owner age or a code-specific succession shortage; the finalized input correctly treats workforce and owner populations as distinct and keeps the shortage boolean false.

publication_caveats: inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Role mix is supplied at five-digit 62134 rather than six-digit 621340.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Clinical-task fractions exclude a claim that AI replaces billable hands-on treatment.
inputs.ai_replaceable_share: Audiology and other residual subsegments may have different exposure.
inputs.pi_dist: CMS Medicare mechanics are a direct payer channel but do not describe every commercial or Medicaid contract.
inputs.pi_moat: Sector consolidation evidence does not prove moat for a clinic whose referrals are physician-personal or payer-concentrated.
inputs.t50_years: Vendor survey language indicates direction, not a representative effective-adoption series.
inputs.current_adoption_pct: No reopened source provides a code-specific percentage of clinics using AI effectively.
inputs.historical_analogs: The source is a vendor survey release and not a causal clinical-productivity study.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct: Workforce age and owner age are different populations.
inputs.owners_60plus_pct.succession_shortage_documented: Do not convert therapist vacancy evidence into owner-succession evidence.
inputs.active_consolidators: Platform count does not establish active acquisition appetite for every local specialty or payer mix.

inputs.buy_mult: S4 comparables are materially larger and mostly historical, so this is a conservative bounded estimate rather than an observed band-size transaction multiple. Verify local transaction data and normalize owner-therapist pay.
inputs.exit_mult: The cited transactions had $12M-$175M EBITDA and cannot support a precise multiple for a smaller clinic group.
inputs.pricing_structure: CMS is not a full description of commercial, Medicaid, school or audiology economics.
summary: Mechanics and exact-artifact binding pass. Reopened sources support the payer-capture channel, administrative AI surface, licensed clinical floor, staffing pressure and active consolidation direction, while the scorecard keeps clinical replacement, capture and terminal demand separate. The pass thesis remains useful, but adoption and small-clinic multiple values are disclosed estimates anchored to vendor evidence and much larger historical transactions, so all authored caveats must remain visible.
